aliases: HPB endorsed no added sugar coffee

english: HPB endorsed no added sugar coffee

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general coffee beverage class; no specific iced Americano term exists. closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers black coffee broadly; iced Americano is a diluted espresso variant served cold. closest_parent is the most defensible mapping.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34731007 refers to coffee as a substance; no specific iced Americano concept exists. closest_parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard tall cup of iced Caffè Americano, approximately 355 ml, consisting of two espresso shots diluted with cold water and served over ice, with no added sugar or milk.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, coffee, brewed, espresso, restaurant-prepared (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, FDC ID 171891) and Beverages, water, tap, drinking (FDC ID 174831); values blended proportionally for a 2-shot Americano diluted to 355 ml
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: An iced Caffè Americano is espresso diluted with water and served over ice with no added sugar or milk. USDA FDC SR Legacy entry for brewed espresso (FDC ID 171891) was used as the espresso base. A standard tall Americano uses approximately 60 ml espresso diluted to 355 ml total with water. Per-100g values were calculated by proportional blending: espresso contributes ~17% of total volume, water ~83%. This yields a very low-calorie beverage. The HPB Singapore endorsement of no added sugar is consistent with these values. Starbucks Singapore nutritional disclosure for a Tall Iced Caffè Americano (no milk, no syrup) reports approximately 10 kcal per serving (355 ml), consistent with the USDA-derived estimate.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; black coffee and espresso-based beverages without sugar have negligible glycemic impact
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for iced Caffè Americano. Black coffee and espresso without added sugar contain negligible fermentable carbohydrates and have no meaningful glycemic response. Per University of Sydney GI Database convention and peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al. 2021), unsweetened black coffee is assigned GI = 0 in practice; however, per Veritas zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel) to satisfy numeric validation requirements. gi_category is low. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate_g per serving is effectively zero.

hypertension.key_factor: Negligible sodium content; caffeine may cause transient blood pressure elevation
hypertension.guidance: An iced Caffè Americano contains approximately 7 mg sodium per 355 ml serving and poses no dietary sodium concern for hypertensive individuals; however, caffeine (approximately 150 mg per tall serving) may cause a transient rise in blood pressure and should be moderated in caffeine-sensitive hypertensive patients.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: No added sugar; negligible carbohydrate content; HPB-endorsed no added sugar
type2_diabetes.guidance: This beverage contains no added sugar and fewer than 2 g of carbohydrate per 355 ml serving, making it suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without added syrups or sweeteners; it carries the HPB Singapore Healthier Choice endorsement for no added sugar.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero saturated fat; zero cholesterol; moderate caffeine intake
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: An iced Caffè Americano without milk or sugar contains no saturated fat or cholesterol and is consistent with a heart-healthy dietary pattern; moderate coffee consumption (up to 3–4 cups per day) has been associated with neutral to beneficial cardiovascular outcomes in epidemiological studies, though individuals with arrhythmias should monitor caffeine intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: An Iced Caffè Americano is a cold coffee beverage made by pulling one or more shots of espresso, diluting them with cold water, and serving the mixture over ice. The result is a long, refreshing black coffee drink with a rich espresso character, mild bitterness, and subtle roasted depth, without the concentrated intensity of straight espresso. At Starbucks Singapore, a Tall Iced Caffè Americano typically uses two espresso shots diluted to approximately 355 ml. The drink contains no milk, no added sugar, and no syrups in its standard form, making it one of the lowest-calorie coffee options available. It carries the Health Promotion Board (HPB) Singapore Healthier Choice endorsement for no added sugar. Nutritionally, the beverage is extremely low in energy (approximately 10 kcal per 355 ml serving), contains negligible carbohydrate, fat, and protein, and provides a modest amount of potassium and B vitamins derived from the espresso. The caffeine content is approximately 150 mg per tall serving, which is relevant for individuals managing caffeine intake. The glycemic index is effectively zero as the beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates. It is suitable for vegetarians, vegans, and those following halal or gluten-free diets. Clinically, it is appropriate for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease when consumed without added sugar or high-fat dairy, though caffeine-sensitive individuals should moderate intake.
common_user_queries: How many calories in an iced Americano?
Is iced Americano suitable for diabetics?
What is the difference between iced Americano and iced coffee?
How much caffeine in a Starbucks iced Americano?
Can I drink iced Americano if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: No direct HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN entry exists for iced Caffè Americano as a prepared beverage. Nutrient values were derived by proportional adaptation from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy espresso data (FDC ID 171891), blended with water at the standard Americano dilution ratio. The resulting per-100g values are consistent with Starbucks Singapore's published nutritional disclosure for a Tall Iced Caffè Americano. Confidence is medium because the values are adapted rather than directly measured from a primary regional source.
notes: 1. fat_g is 0.0: naturally zero — an iced Caffè Americano made with espresso and water contains no fat by composition; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy espresso entry (FDC ID 171891). 2. saturated_fat_g is 0.0: naturally zero by product composition. 3. trans_fat_g is 0.0: naturally zero. 4. monounsaturated_fat_g is 0.0: naturally zero. 5. polyunsaturated_fat_g is 0.0: naturally zero. 6. sugar_g is 0.0: confirmed zero — no added sugar; HPB Singapore Healthier Choice endorsement for no added sugar. 7. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: naturally zero for a liquid coffee beverage. 8. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: naturally zero — no animal fat present. 9. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per Veritas zero-calorie beverage glycemic convention. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate per serving is effectively zero. 10. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg and vitamin_b3_niacin_mg are non-zero trace values derived from espresso base per USDA FDC. 11. potassium_mg 49.0 per 100g is derived from espresso dilution; espresso is a known source of potassium. 12. Starbucks Singapore Tall Iced Caffè Americano (no milk, no syrup) reports approximately 10 kcal per 355 ml serving, consistent with the USDA-derived per-100g estimate of 2.8 kcal. 13. The HPB Singapore description endorses this drink as no added sugar. 14. Caffeine content is approximately 150 mg per tall (355 ml) serving based on standard Starbucks espresso dosing; caffeine is not a tracked nutrient in this schema. 15. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg are 0.0 as these are confirmed zero or below detection threshold in diluted espresso per USDA FDC SR Legacy.

Applied zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule: glycemic_index set to 1 (not null or 0), gi_category set to 'low', glycemic_load_per_serving set to 0.0, documented in notes and gi_source.
Applied aliases consistency rule: verified every aliases_by_language entry appears verbatim in flat aliases list.

Applied sentinel values documentation rule: all 0.0 values in per_100g that represent confirmed zero are documented in notes with justification phrases.
